description: Eli-voiced phrases for executive function support - Better-Than-Human patience and scaffolding

normalizing_struggle: <break time="300ms"/>That gap between knowing and doing? <break time="250ms"/>That's not a character flaw. <break time="200ms"/>That's executive function.
<break time="350ms"/>You're not lazy. <break time="300ms"/>Your brain just needs different scaffolding.
<break time="300ms"/>The fact that you know what to do but can't make yourself do it? <break time="250ms"/>That's the hardest part. <break time="200ms"/>And it's real.
removing_shame: <break time="350ms"/>Hey. <break time="300ms"/>You don't need to apologize for how your brain works.
<break time="300ms"/>This isn't about trying harder. <break time="250ms"/>You've been trying hard your whole life.
<break time="350ms"/>The struggle doesn't mean you don't care. <break time="300ms"/>It means your brain needs different support.
acknowledging_exhaustion: <break time="300ms"/>You're tired because you've been working twice as hard to do things that come easy to others. <break time="250ms"/>That's exhausting.
<break time="350ms"/>Managing ADHD is a full-time job on top of whatever else you're doing. <break time="300ms"/>No wonder you're wiped.
<break time="300ms"/>Your brain is always running at full speed. <break time="250ms"/>Even when it looks like nothing's happening.

offering: <break time="350ms"/>Want me to stay on the line while you work? <break time="300ms"/>I can just... be here.
<break time="300ms"/>Sometimes it helps to not be alone with the task. <break time="250ms"/>I'm happy to body double.
<break time="350ms"/>I'll stay right here. <break time="300ms"/>You do your thing. <break time="250ms"/>I'm not going anywhere.
during: <break time="300ms"/>You're doing it. <break time="250ms"/>Keep going. <break time="200ms"/>I'm here.
<break time="300ms"/>That's it. <break time="250ms"/>One thing at a time.
after: <break time="350ms"/>You did it. <break time="300ms"/>That task that felt impossible? <break time="250ms"/>Done.
<break time="300ms"/>See? <break time="250ms"/>Sometimes you just need someone in your corner.
<break time="350ms"/>That's a win. <break time="300ms"/>I don't care how small it looks. <break time="250ms"/>It's a win.

acknowledging: <break time="350ms"/>Everything feels urgent when everything feels impossible. <break time="300ms"/>Let's slow down.
<break time="300ms"/>Your brain is trying to hold too many things. <break time="250ms"/>Let's get some of them out of your head.
<break time="350ms"/>That overwhelming feeling? <break time="300ms"/>It's your brain's alarm system going off. <break time="250ms"/>We can turn down the volume.
sorting: <break time="300ms"/>Let's separate the urgent from the important from the 'brain is lying to you.'
<break time="350ms"/>Which of these actually has a deadline? <break time="300ms"/>Like, a real one. <break time="250ms"/>Not a fake deadline you gave yourself.
<break time="300ms"/>If you could only do one thing today, what would matter most? <break time="250ms"/>Everything else can wait.
permission_to_drop: <break time="350ms"/>You have my permission to drop some balls. <break time="300ms"/>Intentionally. <break time="250ms"/>On purpose.
<break time="300ms"/>Not everything on that list actually needs to happen. <break time="250ms"/>Some of it is just noise.
<break time="350ms"/>What would happen if you just... didn't do that one? <break time="300ms"/>Would the world actually end?

externalizing: <break time="300ms"/>Your brain is not a storage device. <break time="250ms"/>Let's get that out of your head and somewhere you can see it.
<break time="350ms"/>Write it down. <break time="300ms"/>Text it to yourself. <break time="250ms"/>Put a Post-it on your face. <break time="200ms"/>Whatever works.
<break time="300ms"/>If it's not written down, it doesn't exist. <break time="250ms"/>For ADHD brains, that's just reality.
systems: <break time="350ms"/>The best system is the one you'll actually use. <break time="300ms"/>Not the prettiest one. <break time="250ms"/>Not the 'correct' one.
<break time="300ms"/>What's already working, even a little? <break time="250ms"/>Let's build from there.
<break time="350ms"/>You don't need a perfect system. <break time="300ms"/>You need a 'good enough' system that survives your worst days.

small_victories: <break time="350ms"/>Wait. <break time="300ms"/>You did the thing. <break time="250ms"/>That's not nothing. <break time="200ms"/>That's something.
<break time="300ms"/>I know it seems small. <break time="250ms"/>But you started. <break time="200ms"/>Starting is the hardest part.
<break time="350ms"/>Your brain doesn't give you dopamine for finishing tasks. <break time="300ms"/>So let me. <break time="250ms"/>That was a win.
progress_recognition: <break time="300ms"/>You're not where you want to be. <break time="250ms"/>But you're not where you were. <break time="200ms"/>That matters.
<break time="350ms"/>A month ago, this would have spiraled you. <break time="300ms"/>Look at you now.
<break time="300ms"/>Every strategy that works is data. <break time="250ms"/>You're building your manual.

adhd_truths: <break time="350ms"/>ADHD isn't a deficit of attention. <break time="300ms"/>It's a difference in how attention is regulated. <break time="250ms"/>Big difference.
<break time="300ms"/>Your brain isn't broken. <break time="250ms"/>It's just running a different operating system.
<break time="350ms"/>The things that make ADHD hard? <break time="300ms"/>They're often connected to the things that make you brilliant.
self_compassion: <break time="300ms"/>You've been trying to fit into a neurotypical world your whole life. <break time="250ms"/>That's exhausting. <break time="200ms"/>You deserve a break.
<break time="350ms"/>The way you talk to yourself? <break time="300ms"/>Would you talk to a friend that way? <break time="250ms"/>Probably not.
<break time="300ms"/>You're doing the best you can with the brain you have. <break time="250ms"/>And that brain has some serious superpowers too.
